edges.evidence_quote: In 1985, the National Liberation Front of Kurdistan (ERNK) was established by the PKK as its popular front wing.

descriptions: Longsight Crew is a junior faction of Doddington.
edges.evidence_quote: Doddington gave rise to the Moss Side Bloods , the Longsight Crew, the Haydock Close Crew and the Portuguese Mafia.

descriptions: Fallowfield Mandem inherited conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: The other FMD members were subsequently drawn into gang culture, allying themselves with the Gooch and inheriting their war with Doddington and Longsight.

descriptions: Fallowfield Mad Dogs were in a feud with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: He was ambushed by members of a younger faction of the Longsight Crew near his home and killed by a shotgun blast. His death was part of a vicious feud between the FMD (who wore blue and were tied to Gooch) and the newer "L-Town" faction of the Longsight Crew

descriptions: Gooch Close Gang had violent disputes with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gooch gang had violent disputes with many other South Manchester gangs, most notably ... the Longsight Crew

descriptions: Longsight Crew and Gooch gang were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2004, members of the Gooch gang and Longsight Crew fought each other in the Manchester Royal Infirmary .

descriptions: Longsight Street Soldiers split from Longsight Crew and became hostile.
edges.evidence_quote: The LSSS (L triple S) were originally a faction of the Longsight Crew, but they became disillusioned after a dispute escalated to tit-for-tat shootings.

descriptions: Old Trafford Cripz were in conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: Trading shots with ... the LSC

descriptions: Pit Bull Crew was in conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: The PBC waged war on the Longsight Crew and the Doddington

descriptions: Young Gooch was in conflict with Longsight Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: The Young Gooch started to war with the newly emerging Longsight Crew

'Ndrina Serpa.description: The 'Ndrina Serpa is a Mafia organization linked to the 'Ndrangheta, operating in the province of Cosenza and originating from Paola, Italy. Its activities include drug and arms trafficking, illegal waste disposal, public contract rigging, extortion, and usury. Founded by Mario Serpa, the clan has been led by various family members, including Nella Serpa, who took over after her brother's murder. The organization has been involved in violent feuds with rival clans.

descriptions: Muto and Serpa are listed together in the 'tela di ragno' operation.
edges.evidence_quote: Le 'ndrine in questione sono: Muto di Cetraro, ... Scofano-Martello-Ditto-La Rosa e Serpa di Paola

descriptions: The Serpa clan allied with the Perna faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Con i Perna si allearono anche gli Africano di Amantea e i Serpa di Paola

descriptions: Serpa allied with Perna.
edges.evidence_quote: e i Serpa di Paola

descriptions: 'Ndrina Serpa is a mafia organization linked to 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina o cosca Serpa è un'organizzazione mafiosa legata alla 'ndrangheta

descriptions: Scofano-Martello-La Rosa killed the boss of 'Ndrina Serpa, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Francescopio Serpe, boss della famiglia negli anni '90 e nei primi anni '2000, prima di essere ucciso dai rivali Scofano-Martello-La Rosa il 27 maggio 2003

descriptions: Serpa and Martello were opposing sides in the Faida di Paola.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Paola: faida in atto tra il clan Serpa contro i Martello per il controllo del territorio di Paola

descriptions: Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen militant force loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.

descriptions: Titanic Stones are a splinter group that split from BPSN.
edges.evidence_quote: The Titanic Stones were supporters of Eugene Hairston who had a falling-out with Fort.

Nakano-kai.description: The Nakano-kai was a yakuza gang based in Osaka, Japan, founded by Taro Nakano after World War II. It was known for its fierce bellicosity and was originally an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi. In 1997, it broke from the Yamaguchi-gumi by assassinating a high-ranking member, leading to a police crackdown and the gang's disbandment.

descriptions: Nakano-kai assassinated the head of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was shot and killed ... by members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Nakano-kai attacked Yamaguchi-gumi, assassinating its number 2, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: But in July of that year, the Nakano-kai broke spectacularly from its parent in a violent attack that led to the group's disbanding.

descriptions: Nakano-kai is a breakaway affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Nakano-kai was an affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi before 1997.
edges.evidence_quote: Before 1997, the Nakano-kai had been an affiliate of the Kobe -based Yamaguchi-gumi , Japan's largest yakuza group.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi retaliated against Nakano-kai after the assassination of Masaru Takumi.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997 when Takumi was assassinated by the Nakano-kai, he succeeded as the head of the Takumi-gumi, retaliating ruthlessly against the Nakano-kai.

descriptions: Members of Nakano-kai assassinated the leader of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was kumicho of yakuza's 1000-members until his assassination in Kobe in 1997 by members of the Nakano-kai .

descriptions: In the 1990s, Albanese allied with Cutellè and Tassone in a faida against Ferrentino, Chindamo, Lamari, and D'Agostino.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

descriptions: The Mancuso helped the Cutellè in a feud against the Chindamo.
edges.evidence_quote: il boss Giuseppe Mancuso per aiutare la 'ndrina dei Cutellè di Laureana di Borrello in faida con i Chindamo ordina l'omicidio di Vincenzo e Antonio Chindamo

Los Metros.description: Los Metros is a Mexican criminal organization and a splinter faction of the Gulf Cartel, based in Reynosa, Tamaulipas. It was initially created by Mario Cárdenas Guillén and later led by Samuel Flores Borrego. The group is involved in drug trafficking, homicide, money laundering, and other crimes. It remains active and is one of five Gulf Cartel factions vying for control in Tamaulipas.

descriptions: Cártel del Noreste lists Los Metros as enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Chapitos , Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación , Los Metros , Cártel de Caborca , Los Ciclones , Cártel de Juárez , La Familia Michoacana , Zetas Vieja Escuela

descriptions: Infobox lists Los Metros as an enemy of Cártel del Noreste.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Metros

descriptions: Los Metros split from Cártel del Golfo, starting an internal war.
edges.evidence_quote: El liderazgo de la organización recayó en Homero Cárdenas Guillén hasta su presunta muerte; durante su liderazgo, el grupo de Los Metros se deslindó de la organización, iniciando una guerra interna en el cártel

descriptions: Metros is a faction within the Gulf Cartel, involved in internal conflict with the Rojos faction.
edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010

descriptions: The Gulf Cartel created Los Metros as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como ... Los Metros

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Metros as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como Los Ciclones , Los Metros , Los Escorpiones , Los Rojos , Los Sierra o División Sierra , Los Ceros, Fuerzas Especiales Grupo Sombra (F.E.G.S.), Los Zetas , entre otros.

edges.evidence_quote: Fue en 2010 cuando Los Zetas rompieron su pacto con el Cartel Del Golfo y fue cuando se desató la ola de violencia en Tamaulipas, y poco después cuando comenzó la guerra interna, facciones del Cartel Del Golfo y de Los Zetas se enfrentan entre sí

descriptions: Los Rojos ordered the assassination of a Metros leader, triggering conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 September 2011, Mejía González and Rafael Cárdenas Vela , two leaders of the Rojos, ordered the assassination of the drug lord Samuel Flores Borrego , who commanded the Metros, another faction within the Gulf cartel.

descriptions: Grupo Bravo is in conflict with Los Metros.
edges.evidence_quote: El Grupo Bravo se encuentra, actualmente, en conflicto con Los Ciclones y Los Metros (también del Cártel del Golfo)

Tenth Avenue Gang.description: New York City street gang formed by Ike Marsh in 1860, later becoming the city's first train robbers. They committed a notable train robbery in Manhattan in 1868, stealing $5,000 in cash and bonds. The gang merged with the Hell's Kitchen Gang in the early 1870s after several members were killed by railroad detectives.

descriptions: The Tenth Avenue Gang merged with the Hell's Kitchen Gang in the early 1870s.
edges.evidence_quote: by the early 1870s the gang was forced to merge with the Hell's Kitchen Gang

Bufalino crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Northeastern Pennsylvania, primarily in Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, and Pittston. Originated in the 1880s with Stefano La Torre, involved in labor racketeering in the coal and garment industries. Longest-serving boss Russell Bufalino led until his death in 1994. The family was active from around 1900 until 2008.

Dnipropetrovsk Mafia.description: A group of Ukrainian oligarchs, politicians, and organized crime figures based in Dnipro (formerly Dnipropetrovsk), Ukraine. It emerged in the aftermath of the Great Purge and was firmly established in the 1950s-1960s under Leonid Brezhnev. The group wielded powerful influence in Soviet and post-Soviet Ukraine, with members involved in black market activities. It declined after Brezhnev's death and Gorbachev's rise.

descriptions: The Dnipropetrovsk Mafia and Donetsk Clan were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: These events precipitated the conflict between the Dnipropetrovsk Mafia, led by Kuchma, and the Donetsk Clan, led by Zvyahilsky.

descriptions: The Kyiv clan supported Kuchma against Lazarenko, cooperating with the Dnipropetrovsk Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Kuchma's efforts to stamp out Lazarenko were supported by both the remnants of the Donetsk clan and the Kyiv clan, another oligarch group.

descriptions: Ergenekon colluded with the PKK.
edges.evidence_quote: Former police special forces member Ayhan Çarkın said that the state, using the clandestine Ergenekon network, colluded with militant groups such as the PKK, Dev-Sol and Turkish Hezbollah, with the goal of profiting from the war.

descriptions: The Yüksekova Gang had possible links to Ergenekon.
edges.evidence_quote: The Yüksekova Gang was an illegal organization in the Yüksekova district of Hakkari Province accused of killing 16 people, with apparent links to the Turkish Gendarmerie 's JITEM unit and possible links to the Ergenekon organization.

Manitoba Warriors.description: The Manitoba Warriors are an indigenous street gang based in Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada, founded in 1993 at Stony Mountain Institution. The gang is exclusively Indigenous Canadian and was established to rival the Indian Posse. They engage in drug trafficking, auto theft, illegal firearms activities, gambling, and support Hells Angels and Asian-based networks. Criminal Intelligence Service Canada designates them as a faction of indigenous-based organized crime.

descriptions: Feud between Manitoba Warriors and Hells Angels associates over drug and prostitution rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 1996, three men were shot dead inside a home in West Kildonan, Winnipeg as part of a feud over control of drug and prostitution rackets between members of the Manitoba Warriors and associates of the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Indian Posse and Manitoba Warriors are rivals, engaged in a prison riot.
edges.evidence_quote: On 25 April 1996, a riot broke out at Headingley Correctional Institution between the imprisoned Indian Posse members versus the imprisoned members of the rival Manitoba Warriors

descriptions: Redd Alert was formed as an alternative to joining Manitoba Warriors, indicating a non-cooperative, possibly rivalrous origin.
edges.evidence_quote: Redd Alert was established in 1999 by Robert Lee Wagner along with other First Nations inmates of Cree, Anishinaabe, and Métis origin as an alternative to joining two of the other Aboriginal gangs within the Alberta prison system: the Indian Posse and the Manitoba Warriors.

descriptions: Rock Machine allied with Manitoba Warriors during expansion into Western Canada in 2008.
edges.evidence_quote: A Native-Canadian gang based in the Canada's prairies, an alliance was formed between the two groups during Rock Machine's expansion into Western Canada in 2008.

descriptions: The Ribbon Men are mentioned as one of the gangs among many that formed after Irish migration, similar to the Penny Mobs.
edges.evidence_quote: One of these gangs, the Ribbon Men, were reported to have blown up a gas holder in Tradeston in 1883.

descriptions: Latin Eagles allied with Ashland Vikings in the Folks alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Latin Eagles became enemies of all People gangs when they joined the prison Folks alliance of Larry Hoover and his Black Gangster Disciples, Simon City Royals, Ashland Vikings, Latin Disciples and others in 1978.
